title: Finish the task-tracker CLI (complete + purge)
description: The crate builds and tests pass in their committed state, but `complete` and `purge` in `src/commands.rs` are unimplemented stubs (`todo!()`). Implement them correctly and add tests that cover both functions.
acceptance_criteria: Implement `complete(&mut self, id: u64) -> Result<(), CommandError>` in `src/commands.rs`: find the task by id and mark it done, returning `Ok(())`. If no task has that id, return `Err(CommandError::NotFound(id))`. Calling `complete` on an already-done task returns `Ok(())` (idempotent — no error).

Implement `purge(&mut self) -> usize` in `src/commands.rs`: remove every task whose `done` field is `true`, preserve the relative order of surviving (not-done) tasks, and return the count of tasks removed. A second call when no done tasks remain returns 0.

Add tests (inline `#[cfg(test)]` in `src/commands.rs` or another source file) that cover: `complete` returning `Err(CommandError::NotFound(_))` for a nonexistent id; `purge` retaining not-done tasks in their original insertion order; `purge` returning the correct removed count and returning 0 on a second call.
`cargo test` exits 0 with all tests passing.

files_to_modify.change: Replace the `todo!()` bodies in `complete` and `purge` with correct implementations, and add inline tests.
